name: South African Enterprise
description: VAT, PAYE, B-BBEE, POPIA, and South African business compliance rules

entries.title: South African VAT (15%)
entries.content: South Africa uses a 15% VAT rate. All invoices must show VAT-inclusive and VAT-exclusive amounts. VAT registration is mandatory for businesses with annual turnover exceeding R1,000,000. Formula: vat_amount = line_total * 0.15; total_incl = line_total + vat_amount
entries.code_pattern:
vat_rate = Decimal('0.15')
vat_amount = line_total * vat_rate
total_incl_vat = line_total + vat_amount

entries.title: PAYE Tax Tables
entries.content: Pay-As-You-Earn tax brackets for South Africa. Employers must deduct PAYE from employee salaries based on progressive tax brackets. UIF contribution is 1% employee + 1% employer.

entries.title: B-BBEE Scorecard
entries.content: Broad-Based Black Economic Empowerment scoring: Ownership (25pts), Management Control (19pts), Skills Development (20pts), Enterprise and Supplier Development (40pts + bonus), Socio-Economic Development (5pts).

entries.title: POPIA Data Protection
entries.content: Protection of Personal Information Act requires: explicit consent for data collection, data minimisation, purpose limitation, storage limitation, and right to deletion. All systems must have data subject access request (DSAR) endpoints.

entries.title: SA Banking Integration
entries.content: Major banks: ABSA, FNB, Nedbank, Standard Bank, Capitec. EFT batch files use standard NAEDO/DebiCheck formats. Currency: ZAR (South African Rand).
